title: eriogonum umbellatum subsp. majus
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. majus, also known as the sulfur buckwheat or desert buckwheat, is a perennial shrub native to North America, characterized by its clusters of small, yellow or white flowers and woolly leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing, reducing swelling, and preventing infection. The health benefits of Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. majus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, alleviate respiratory problems, and support immune function, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve making tea, infusions, or tinctures from its dried flowers and leaves, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
